core_model.structure_summary: An equilibrium probability distribution over configurations of binary units with nearest-neighbor pairwise coupling on a regular grid, in which each configuration's weight is an equal mixture of two exponential factors of the coupling energy with two distinct rate parameters, producing a critical curve in the two-parameter plane with power-law scaling on it.
core_model.canonical_form: E(x) = -\sum_{\langle i,j \rangle} x_i x_j, \quad x_i \in \{\pm 1\}; \qquad p(x) \propto \tfrac{1}{2}\left(e^{-\beta_1 E(x)} + e^{-\beta_2 E(x)}\right); \qquad \text{generalized: } p(x) \propto \tfrac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^{n} e^{-\beta_i E(x)}

variables.meaning: array of binary (+1/-1) units on the grid sites
two rate parameters weighting the exponential factors in the mixture; their plane contains the critical curve
linear system size, used for finite-size scaling of the order parameter and fluctuation quantities

notes: The sampling dynamics (single-unit and cluster update rules with modified acceptance/link probabilities) are algorithms constructed to sample the two-rate mixture weight and satisfy detailed balance with respect to it; the paper also derives an effective single-rate mapping for the critical curve and generalizes to n-component mixtures. The core object is the mixture-weight equilibrium distribution itself.
